IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: ENCOURAGEMENT OF PUBLIC INTEREST IN ART.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part XII, Line 2 UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS DISCLOSURE
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MANAGEMENT HAS EVALUATED THEIR INCOME TAX POSITIONS UNDER THE GUIDANCE INCLUDED IN ASC 740. BASED ON THEIR REVIEW, MANAGEMENT HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ANY MATERIAL UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS TO BE RECORDED OR DISCLOSED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part III, Line 1a Collections of art - financial statement footnote
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IN CONFORMITY WITH ACCOUNTING POLICIES GENERALLY FOLLOWED BY ART MUSEUMS, THE COLLECTIONS THAT WERE ACQUIRED THROUGH PURCHASES BY THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES AND CONTRIBUTIONS SINCE THE INCEPTION ARE NOT RECOGNIZED AS ASSETS ON THE CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF FINANCIAL POSITION. PURCHASES OF COLLECTION ITEMS ARE RECORDED AS DECREASES IN NET ASSETS WITHOUT DONOR RESTRICTIONS IN THE YEAR IN WHICH THE ITEMS ARE ACQUIRED OR AS DECREASES IN NET ASSETS WITH DONOR RESTRICTIONS IF THE ASSETS USED TO PURCHASE THE ITEMS WERE RESTRICTED BY DONORS. CONTRIBUTED COLLECTION ITEMS ARE NOT REFLECTED ON THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. PROCEEDS FROM DEACCESSIONS ARE REFLECTED AS INCREASES IN NET ASSETS WITH DONOR RESTRICTIONS. THE MUSEUM MAINTAINS COMPREHENSIVE FINE ARTS COLLECTIONS OF APPROXIMATELY 43,180 AND 42,900 (UNAUDITED) PIECES FOR THE YEARS ENDED APRIL 30, 2023 AND 2022, RESPECTIVELY, SPANNING APPROXIMATELY 5,000 YEARS AND MANY CULTURES. THE RESPECTIVE COLLECTIONS ARE USED FOR THE PURPOSES OF EXHIBITION, EDUCATION, STUDY, RESEARCH, PUBLICATIONS AND LOANS TO OTHER MUSEUMS. THE COLLECTIONS ARE KEPT UNDER CURATORIAL CARE, INCLUDING EXTENSIVE CONSERVATION PRACTICES AND SPECIALIZED FINE ARTS INSURANCE COVERAGE, AND ARE SUBJECT TO THE MUSEUM'S POLICIES THAT REQUIRE PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE OF COLLECTION ITEMS TO BE USED ONLY FOR ACQUISITION OF ADDITIONAL, SAME-COLLECTION ITEMS. ALL COLLECTION ITEMS DEACCESSED DURING THE YEARS ENDED APRIL 30, 2023 AND 2022, WERE PLACED ON CONSIGNMENT WITH ART DEALERS TO BE SOLD IN THE ORDINARY COURSE OF BUSINESS; AS A CONSEQUENCE, SOME DEACCESSIONED ITEMS REMAIN UNSOLD ON CONSIGNMENT AT YEAR-END. NO COLLECTION ITEMS WERE GIVEN AWAY, DESTROYED, LOST OR STOLEN DURING THE YEARS ENDED APRIL 30, 2023 AND 2022.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part III, Line 4 Collections of art - description of collections
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MUSEUM'S COMPREHENSIVE PERMANENT COLLECTION SPANS 5,000 YEARS AND NUMBERS OVER 43,000 OBJECTS. THE ASIAN ART COLLECTION IS AMONG THE FINEST IN THE WORLD BY VIRTUE OF ITS SIZE, DEPTH, AND OVERALL LEVEL OF QUALITY. THE OUTSTANDING ANCIENT ART INCLUDES MASTERPIECES FROM ANCIENT MESOPOTAMIA, EGYPT AND THE CLASSICAL WORLD. THE ARCHITECTURE, DESIGN AND DECORATIVE ARTS COLLECTION CONTAINS EUROPEAN AND AMERICAN HOLDINGS. THE EUROPEAN PAINTING AND SCULPTURE ART COLLECTION IS OF FINE QUALITY THROUGHOUT. THE AMERICAN ART COLLECTION INCLUDES PAINTINGS, SCULPTURES, AND DRAWINGS WITH SUPERB PORTRAITS AND LANDSCAPES BY MAJOR 18TH AND 19TH-CENTURY PAINTERS. THE MODERN AND CONTEMPORARY ART COLLECTION INCLUDES IMPORTANT WORKS FROM THE EARLY 20TH-CENTURY TO THE PRESENT. THE NATIVE AMERICAN ART COLLECTION IS PARTICULARLY STRONG IN SOUTHWEST NAVAJO MATERIAL. THE AFRICAN ART HOLDINGS INCLUDE OVER 400 OBJECTS, AND THE MUSEUM HAS MADE AN INSTITUTIONAL COMMITMENT TO DEVELOP KANSAS CITY AS A SIGNIFICANT CENTER FOR THE COLLECTION AND STUDY OF AFRICAN ART. THE BREADTH, DEPTH, AND QUALITY OF THE MUSEUM'S PHOTOGRAPHY COLLECTION ARE MATCHED BY ONLY A SMALL GROUP OF THE LARGEST MUSEUMS IN THE UNITED STATES. THE PHOTOGRAPHY COLLECTION PROVIDES A PANORAMIC VISION OF ITS SUBJECT, AMERICAN PHOTOGRAPHY, FROM THE INVENTION OF THE MEDIUM IN 1839 TO THE PRESENT. THE MUSEUM IS ALSO HOME OF THE 20 ACRE DONALD J. HALL SCULPTURE PARK WHICH INCLUDES THE LARGEST COLLECTION OF HENRY MOORE SCULPTURES OUTSIDE OF ENGLAND. ANY WORK OF ART NOT ON VIEW IS AVAILABLE FOR RESEARCH, UNLESS ITS CONDITION PRECLUDES THIS USE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part V, Line 4 Intended uses of endowment funds
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ENDOWMENTS AND BOARD DESIGNATED QUASI-ENDOWMENT FUNDS ARE IN PLACE TO SUPPORT SPECIFIC EDUCATION PROGRAMS, EXHIBITIONS, COLLECTIONS ADMINISTRATION, ART ACQUISITIONS, AND OPERATIONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule J, Part I, Line 1a Health or social club dues or initiation fees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE NELSON GALLERY FOUNDATION MAINTAINS A MEMBERSHIP AT THE MISSION HILLS COUNTRY CLUB, A SOCIAL CLUB, IN THE NAME OF THE DIRECTOR/CEO. THIS MEMBERSHIP IS USED BY THE NELSON GALLERY FOUNDATION FOR HOSTING BUSINESS MEETINGS, BUT IS AVAILABLE TO THE DIRECTOR/CEO FOR PERSONAL USE. MEMBERSHIP DUES ARE TREATED AS TAXABLE COMPENSATION TO THE DIRECTOR/CEO.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule J, Part I, Line 4b Supplemental nonqualified retirement plan
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CEO PARTICIPATES IN A DEFERRED COMPENSATION PLAN UNDER 457F. DURING THE YEAR, A CONTRIBUTION OF $100,000 WAS MADE AND THE CEO VESTED $174,554 IN THE 457F PLAN.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part I, Column (f) LINE A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: REFUND SERIES 2001B BONDS ISSUED ON 12/6/2001; CONSTRUCTION AND RENOVATION OF VARIOUS MUSEUM BUILDINGS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part I, Column (f) LINE B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CONSTRUCTION OF BLOCH BUILDING AND IMPROVEMENTS TO EXISTING MUSEUM BUILDING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part I, Column (f) LINE C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: REFUND A PORTION OF THE SERIES 2008A BONDS ISSUED ON 08/28/2008.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part I, Column (g) LINE A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A PORTION OF THE SERIES 2008A BONDS WAS REFUNDED BY THE SERIES 2012A BONDS ON AUGUST 7, 2012.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part II, Line 2 COLUMN A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A PORTION OF THE SERIES 2008A BONDS ($48,000,000) WAS REFUNDED BY THE SERIES 2012A BONDS ON AUGUST 7, 2012.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part II, Line 3 COLUMN A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AMOUNT IS NOT EQUAL TO ISSUE PRICE DUE TO INVESTMENT EARNINGS EARNED DURING THE PROJECT PERIOD. THIS AMOUNT DOES NOT INCLUDE $500,000 OF SALE PROCEEDS USED TO REDEEM BONDS OF THE ISSUE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part II, Line 3 COLUMN B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AMOUNT IS NOT EQUAL TO ISSUE PRICE DUE TO INVESTMENT EARNINGS EARNED DURING THE PROJECT PERIOD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part II, Line 11 COLUMN A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: INCLUDES $85,191,278 IN BOND PROCEEDS THAT WAS USED TO REDEEM THE SERIES 2001B BONDS. THIS AMOUNT DOES NOT INCLUDE $500,000 OF SALE PROCEEDS USED TO REDEEM BONDS OF THE ISSUE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part II, Line 11 COLUMN C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: $48,000,000 WAS USED TO REDEEM A PORTION OF THE OUTSTANDING SERIES 2008A BONDS ON THE ISSUE DATE OF THE SERIES 2012A BONDS. ADDITIONALLY, $14,272.90 OF THE AMOUNT ORIGINALLY DEPOSITED INTO THE COSTS OF ISSUANCE FUND WAS TRANSFERRED TO THE DEBT SERVICE FUND AND USED TO PAY INTEREST ON THE SERIES 2012A BONDS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part III, Line 5 COLUMN (A)(B)(C)
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE INSTITUTION HOLDS VARIOUS EVENTS THAT MAY RESULT IN PRIVATE BUSINESS USE AS A RESULT OF UNRELATED TRADE OR BUSINESS ACTIVITY WITHIN THE MUSEUM. THE MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF POTENTIAL PRIVATE BUSINESS USE THAT MAY RESULT FROM THE UNRELATED TRADE OR BUSINESS ACTIVITY IS 0.5% FOR THE FISCAL YEAR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part IV, Line 2c COLUMN A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL GROSS PROCEEDS WERE SPENT AS OF THE COMPUTATION DATE. ARBITRAGE REBATE ANALYSIS DATED AUGUST 1, 2012.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part IV, Line 5b COLUMN A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ROYAL BANK OF CANADA

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part IV, Line 5b COLUMN B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TRANSAMERICA OCCIDENTAL LIFE INSURANCE CO.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part IV, Line 6 COLUMN A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCEEDS IN THE PROJECT FUND WERE INVESTED BEYOND THE 3-YEAR TEMPORARY PERIOD; HOWEVER, NO EXCESS YIELD LIABILITY WAS GENERATED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule K, Part IV, Line 6 COLUMN B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ANY AMOUNTS INVESTED BEYOND THE 3-YEAR TEMPORARY PERIOD WERE REFLECTED IN THE ARBITRAGE REBATE ANALYSIS DATED APRIL 29, 2009 AND ON THE FORM 8038-T

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I, Line 32b Third parties used to solicit, process, or sell noncash contributions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: USE OF THIRD PARTIES TO PROCESS OR SELL NONCASH CONTRIBUTIONS- AUCTION HOUSES, OR OCCASIONALLY DEALERS, ARE USED TO SELL WORKS DEACCESSIONED FROM THE COLLECTIONS. THESE WORKS INCLUDE GIFTS AND BEQUESTS AS WELL AS PURCHASES. A CURATOR IDENTIFIES A WORK FOR POSSIBLE DEACCESSION. IF THE DIRECTOR/CEO APPROVES DEACCESSIONING THE WORK, THE CURATOR CONTACTS AUCTION HOUSES OR DEALERS TO GET SALE ESTIMATES. THE CURATOR RECOMMENDS AN AUCTION HOUSE OR DEALER TO THE DIRECTOR/CEO. THE CURATOR PREPARES A DEACCESSION PROPOSAL AND PRESENTS THE WORK TO THE COMMITTEE ON COLLECTIONS (THE 'COC'). THE COC HAS THE AUTHORITY TO APPROVE DEACCESSIONS WITH AN ESTIMATED SALE VALUE OF LESS THAN $25,000. THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES MUST APPROVE DEACCESSIONS WITH AN ESTIMATED SALE VALUE OF $25,000 OR MORE. THE DIRECTOR/CEO SIGNS THE AUCTION HOUSE'S OR DEALER'S CONSIGNMENT AGREEMENT. SALE PROCEEDS ARE RESTRICTED TO THE PURCHASE OF WORK IN THE SAME CURATORIAL AREA.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I, Line 33 Noncash contribution amounts not reported
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PLEASE SEE SCHEDULE D, PART III, LINE 1A.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I Explanations of reporting method for number of contributions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Art - Works of art - COMBINATION OF NUMBER OF ITEMS RECEIVED AND NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS Clothing and household goods - NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS Securities - Publicly traded - NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS Food inventory - NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS Other - MISC. ITEMS ITEMS NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS Other - PACKING AND SHIPPING OF ART NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 1 ORGANIZATION'S MISSION
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE NELSON-ATKINS MUSEUM OF ART IS WHERE THE POWER OF ART ENGAGES THE SPIRIT OF COMMUNITY. WE WELCOME AND PROVIDE FREE ADMISSION TO ALL, AND DELIVER UNPARALLELED OPPORTUNITIES TO ENJOY, APPRECIATE AND UNDERSTAND THE FINEST VISUAL ARTS AND THE VARIED CULTURES THEY REPRESENT - ON OUR CAMPUS, ONLINE, AND THROUGHOUT KANSAS CITY. AS AN INSTITUTION OF NATIONAL AND INTERNATIONAL RENOWN, WE ESTABLISH AND ADHERE TO THE HIGHEST PROFESSIONAL STANDARDS AND PRACTICES IN RESEARCH, SCHOLARSHIP, PRESENTATION, INTERPRETATION AND CARE OF OUR COLLECTIONS. WE SERVE AS A VITAL PARTNER IN THE EDUCATIONAL, SOCIAL AND CULTURAL LIFE OF OUR COMMUNITY, USING OUR COLLECTIONS AND PROGRAMS TO BUILD AN ENVIRONMENT WHERE PEOPLE CAN GATHER, SHARE AND CONTEMPLATE THE GREATEST CREATIONS OF HUMANKIND. EVERY DAY, IN ALL WE DO, THE NELSON-ATKINS IS COMMITTED TO NURTURING EXCELLENCE, INSPIRING CREATIVITY AND BUILDING COMMUNITY THROUGH THE POWER OF ART.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 4a PROGRAM SERVICE DESCRIPTION
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE NELSON-ATKINS MUSEUM OF ART'S COLLECTION OF OVER 43,000 OBJECTS SPANS 5,000 YEARS. IT IS CELEBRATED FOR ITS HOLDINGS OF CHINESE ART, CONSIDERED AMONG THE BEST IN THE WORLD, WITH IMPORTANT COLLECTIONS OF JAPANESE AND SOUTH AND SOUTHEAST ASIAN WORKS OF ART. ITS CELEBRATED EUROPEAN PAINTINGS COLLECTION-INCLUDING THE SECOND PAINTING BY VINCENT VAN GOGH PURCHASED BY A US MUSEUM-CONTAIN ONE OF THE FEW CARAVAGGIO PAINTINGS IN NORTH AMERICA, AS WELL AS MASTERPIECES OF IMPRESSIONIST ART. THE PHOTOGRAPHY COLLECTION IS ONE OF THE FINEST IN THE COUNTRY, SPANNING THE ENTIRE HISTORY OF THE MEDIUM. THE ACCLAIMED NATIVE AMERICAN COLLECTIONS ARE AMONG THE OLDEST IN THE US. THERE ARE ALSO IMPORTANT COLLECTIONS OF EIGHTEENTH CENTURY EUROPEAN DECORATIVE ARTS, AMERICAN PAINT AND SCULPTURE, AND AFRICAN ART, COMPLEMENTED BY THE 22 ACRE DONALD J. HALL SCULPTURE PARK, WHICH ACTS AS ONE OF THE CITY'S FRONT LAWNS AND IS THE SITE OF FREE, POPULAR FESTIVALS CELEBRATING JUNETEENTH, SOUTH AND SOUTHEAST ASIAN HERITAGE, AND LUNAR NEW YEAR, AMONG OTHER COMMUNITIES. IN RECENT YEARS, THE MUSEUM HAS DRAMATICALLY INCREASED ITS CONTEMPORARY COLLECTIONS WITH A DELIBERATE CAMPAIGN OF INCLUSIVITY, ACQUIRING IMPORTANT WORKS IN ALL MEDIA BY BIPOC MAKERS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 2 Family/business relationships amongst interested persons
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PAUL DEBRUCE & LEO MORTON - Business relationship

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 4 Significant changes to organizational documents
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S BYLAWS WERE AMENDED TO EXPAND THE BOARD FROM NOT MORE THAN 24 TRUSTEES TO NOT MORE THAN 28 TRUSTEES. ALSO, THE CHAIRS OF EACH STANDING COMMITTEE SHALL NOW SERVE FOR A TERM OF 3 YEARS RATHER THAN A TERM OF 2 YEARS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 8b Documentation of meetings held by committees of governing body
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MINUTES ARE TAKEN FOR ALL COMMITTEES EXCEPT NOMINATING AND GOVERNANCE. A REPORT IS GIVEN AT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEE MEETINGS AND INCLUDED IN THOSE MEETING MINUTES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 11b Review of form 990 by governing body
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: INITIAL COMPILATION OF THE INFORMATION FOR THE RETURN IS COMPLETED BY FINANCE DEPARTMENT STAFF WITH REVIEW BY THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER PRIOR TO TRANSMISSION TO THE ACCOUNTING FIRM FOR RETURN PREPARATION. UPON RECEIPT OF COMPLETED RETURN AND REVIEW BY THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER THE RETURN WILL BE PROVIDED TO THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER, AUDIT COMMITTEE AND TO ALL TRUSTEES FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 12c Conflict of interest policy
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY REQUIRES THE COMPLETION OF A WRITTEN QUESTIONNAIRE BY EACH DIRECTOR, OFFICER, MEMBER OF A COMMITTEE WITH BOARD-DELEGATED POWER AND KEY EMPLOYEES, ON AN ANNUAL BASIS. THE QUESTIONNAIRE ADDRESSES EACH INDIVIDUAL'S ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF THE RECEIPT OF THE CODE OF CONDUCT AND THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND THEIR COMPLIANCE WITH IT, ALONG WITH REQUIRING RESPONSES TO DETAILED QUESTIONS RELATED TO SPECIFIC AREAS THAT COULD RAISE A CONFLICT. THE BOARD CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES WOULD REVIEW ANY IDENTIFIED CONFLICTS OF INTEREST AND DETERMINE WHETHER ANY FURTHER ACTION WOULD NEED TO OCCUR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 15a Process to establish compensation of top management official
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE AND BOARD CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES REVIEWS THE COMPENSATION AND EMPLOYMENT BENEFITS OF THE DIRECTOR/CEO ON AN ANNUAL BASIS. A VARIETY OF SALARY SURVEY INFORMATION IS PROVIDED BY THE V.P., HUMAN RESOURCES DURING THIS PROCESS INCLUDING SURVEYS BY AMERICAN ASSOCIATION OF MUSEUM DIRECTORS, THE AMERICAN ALLIANCE OF MUSEUMS, SOCIETY FOR HUMAN RESOURCES MANAGEMENT, AND THE NON-PROFIT TIMES SALARY AND BENEFIT REPORT. A COMPENSATION STUDY WAS PERFORMED BY SULLIVAN COTTER WHO THEN PROVIDED A RECOMMENDATION. DURING THE FISCAL YEAR, THE BOARD CHAIR, EVELYN CRAFT BELGER, PROVIDED NOTICE OF THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE REVIEW AND APPROVAL OF THE COMPENSATION TERMS FOR THE FYE APRIL 30, 2023.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 19 Required documents available to the public
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ARE NOT GENERALLY MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC. AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS FOR THE PRIOR YEAR ARE AVAILABLE ON THE ORGANIZATION'S WEBSITE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part IX, Line 11g Other Fees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: OUTSIDE CONTRACTORS - Total Expense: 2518823, Program Service Expense: 1970418, Management and General Expenses: 307909, Fundraising Expenses: 240496; CONTRACT LABOR/FOOD SERVICE - Total Expense: 1124686, Program Service Expense: 879817, Management and General Expenses: 137485, Fundraising Expenses: 107384; OTHER PROFESSIONAL FEES - Total Expense: 1525096, Program Service Expense: 1193048, Management and General Expenses: 186433, Fundraising Expenses: 145615;

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part XI, Line 9 Other changes in net assets or fund balances
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CHANGE IN VALUE OF TRUSTS HELD BY OTHERS - -658792; ACQUISITION AND DISPOSITION OF ART - -305862;

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part XII, Line 2c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE AUDIT COMMITTEE IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ENGAGING THE AUDIT FIRM AND REVIEW OF THE DRAFT AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS BEFORE ISSUANCE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, BOX B REASON FOR AMENDING
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 IS BEING AMENDED TO REFLECT AMENDMENTS TO AN OFFICER'S W-2.
